Dart Fundamentals.aiRubric: Check variables, functions, OOP, null safety, and async/await.

lessons.title: Variables & Data Types in Dart
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Profile Setup
Create variables for `username` (String), `score` (int), and `isActive` (bool). Print them.

lessons.title: Functions & Arrow Syntax
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Arrow Function
Create a function `subtract` that takes two integers and uses arrow syntax `=>` to return their difference.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Dart (Classes & Mixins)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Class with Mixin
Create a class `Player` with a `name` field, and use a `Swimmer` mixin that provides a `swim()` method.

lessons.title: Null Safety
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Nullable String
Create a nullable string `nickname`. Use the null-coalescing operator `??` to print 'No Nickname' if it is null.
lessons.rubric: Nullable type used and null handled.

lessons.title: Asynchronous Dart (Futures & Async/Await)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Async Fetch
Write an async function `getUser` that returns a `Future<String>` 'Ada' after a 1-second delay.

lessons.title: Core Concepts of Dart Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Understanding Dart Fundamentals\nBefore diving into complex implementations, it's essential to understand the foundational principles of Dart Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Define Core Concept\n1. Create a function `get_concept()` that returns the string 'Dart Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.hint: Return the exact string 'Dart Fundamentals Foundation'.
lessons.rubric: Function returns the correct concept string.

lessons.title: Setting up the Environment for Dart Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Tooling\nProperly configuring your development environment is the first step in mastering Dart Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment Status\n1. Create a function `is_ready()` that returns True.

lessons.title: Basic Syntax and Operations
lessons.theory: ## Syntax Rules\nEvery discipline has its syntax. Here we apply standard operations relevant to Dart Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Basic Math\n1. Return the sum of a and b.
lessons.starterCode: def add(a, b):\n    return a ___ b
lessons.solution: def add(a, b):\n    return a + b

lessons.title: Control Structures in Dart Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Branching\nConditional logic dictates how applications respond to different states.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Check State\n1. Return 'Active' if state is 1, else 'Inactive'.

lessons.title: Advanced Memory Management
lessons.theory: ## Resource Allocation\nEfficient memory use is critical, especially when applying Dart Fundamentals at scale.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clear Memory\n1. Set the variable `cache` to an empty dictionary.

lessons.title: Object-Oriented Approaches
lessons.theory: ## OOP\nEncapsulating state and behavior within objects helps structure Dart Fundamentals projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Create Class\n1. Create a class `Module` with a property `name` initialized to 'Dart Fundamentals'.

lessons.title: Functional Programming Paradigms
lessons.theory: ## Pure Functions\nFunctional programming avoids side effects. This is increasingly popular in Dart Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pure Function\n1. Write a function `square(x)` that returns x squared.

lessons.title: Error Handling and Debugging
lessons.theory: ## Resilience\nHandling exceptions gracefully ensures robustness in Dart Fundamentals.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Try/Except\n1. Wrap the division in a try block, catch ZeroDivisionError, and return None.

lessons.title: Advanced Debugging in Dart Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Debugging at Scale\nFinding hard-to-reproduce bugs requires structured methodology.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Debug Setup\n1. Return True for debugging active.

lessons.title: Profiling Dart Fundamentals Applications
lessons.theory: ## Performance Profiling\nIdentifying bottlenecks is the first step in optimization.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Profile Scope\n1. Set `profile_mode` to 'High'.

lessons.title: Memory Leaks and Management
lessons.theory: ## Garbage Collection\nUnderstanding how memory is freed is critical.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: GC Status\n1. Set `gc_enabled` to True.

lessons.title: Multi-threading Constructs
lessons.theory: ## Parallelism\nUtilizing multiple cores improves execution speed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Thread Count\n1. Set `threads` to 8.

lessons.title: Final Project: Dart Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Capstone\nApply all learned concepts to build a comprehensive solution.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Project Initialization\n1. Set `project_name` to 'Dart Fundamentals Final'.

Flutter UI & Widgets.aiRubric: Check stateless/stateful widgets, layout structure, lists, forms, and navigation.

lessons.title: Introduction to Widgets (Stateless vs Stateful)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Simple Widget
Create a `StatelessWidget` called `Greeting` that returns a `Text` widget displaying 'Welcome'.

lessons.title: Layouts (Row, Column, Container)
lessons.theory:
## Layouts
`Container` adds padding/margins. `Row` and `Column` arrange children horizontally and vertically.
```dart
Column(
  children: [ Text('A'), Text('B') ],
)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Column Layout
Return a `Column` containing two `Text` widgets: 'Top' and 'Bottom'.
lessons.hint: Use Column and its children property.

lessons.title: ListView & GridView
lessons.theory:
## Scrollable Lists
Use `ListView.builder` for long scrolling lists.
```dart
ListView.builder(
  itemCount: 10,
  itemBuilder: (context, index) => Text('Item $index'),
)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: List of Names
Create a `ListView.builder` that displays 5 items with the text 'User 0' through 'User 4'.

lessons.title: User Input & Forms
lessons.theory:
## Input
`TextField` allows text entry. Use a `TextEditingController` to read the value.
```dart
final controller = TextEditingController();
TextField(controller: controller)
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Text Field
Create a `TextField` and attach it to a given controller.

lessons.title: Navigation & Routing
lessons.theory:
## Navigation
Use `Navigator.push` to go to a new screen, and `Navigator.pop` to go back.
```dart
Navigator.push(context, MaterialPageRoute(builder: (context) => NextScreen()));
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Push Route
Write the code to navigate to `DetailScreen` when a button is pressed.

lessons.title: Concurrency and Async Operations
lessons.theory: ## Asynchronous Work\nBlocking operations freeze applications. Async allows overlapping tasks in Flutter UI & Widgets.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Async Function\n1. Define an async function `fetch_data()`.

lessons.title: Working with Data Structures
lessons.theory: ## Data Organization\nChoosing the right data structure dictates performance.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Use a Set\n1. Return a set containing the elements 1, 2, 3.

lessons.title: Network Communication Protocols
lessons.theory: ## Connectivity\nUnderstanding HTTP/TCP is essential for networked components in Flutter UI & Widgets.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: HTTP Status\n1. Return 200 for OK.

lessons.title: File I/O and Storage
lessons.theory: ## Persistence\nSaving data to disk allows state to survive reboots.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Open File\n1. Write code to open 'data.txt' in read mode.

lessons.title: Security Best Practices
lessons.theory: ## Defense\nProtecting applications from vulnerabilities is non-negotiable.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Hash Length\n1. Return 64, the length of a SHA-256 hash in hex.

lessons.title: Performance Optimization Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Efficiency\nOptimizing critical paths improves throughput in Flutter UI & Widgets.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Time Complexity\n1. Return 'O(1)' for constant time complexity.

lessons.title: Unit Testing Fundamentals
lessons.theory: ## Validation\nUnit tests verify that individual components work as expected.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Assert True\n1. Write an assert statement that 1 equals 1.

lessons.title: Integration Testing
lessons.theory: ## System Harmony\nIntegration tests ensure that components function together.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Integration Status\n1. Set `integrated` to True.

Flutter State Management.aiRubric: Check setState, Provider/Riverpod usage, HTTP requests, and SharedPreferences.

lessons.title: Local State with setState
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Counter Increment
Inside a `StatefulWidget`'s state class, write the `increment` method that increments `_count` by 1 inside `setState`.

lessons.title: InheritedWidget & Provider Basics
lessons.theory:
## Provider
Provider simplifies InheritedWidget, making data available to the widget tree.
```dart
Provider.of<MyModel>(context).doSomething();
```
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Read Provider Data
Read a `String` from the Provider context and display it in a `Text` widget.

lessons.title: State Management with Riverpod
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Read Riverpod Provider
Use a `ConsumerWidget` to read the `counterProvider` using `ref.watch`.

lessons.title: API Integration (HTTP GET/POST)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: HTTP GET
Write an async function that performs a GET request to 'https://api.mabel.com/users' and returns the response body.

lessons.title: Local Storage (SharedPreferences)
lessons.instructions:
## Task: Save and Read Token
Save the string 'abc123' under the key 'token', then retrieve it.

lessons.title: Continuous Integration Setup
lessons.theory: ## CI Pipelines\nCI automatically runs tests when code is pushed.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Pipeline Step\n1. Create a list with 'build', 'test', 'deploy'.

lessons.title: Deployment Pipelines
lessons.theory: ## Shipping Code\nDeploying reliably requires automated release pipelines.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Environment\n1. Set `env` to 'production'.

lessons.title: Logging and Monitoring
lessons.theory: ## Observability\nLogs help diagnose issues in production.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Log Level\n1. Set `level` to 'ERROR'.

lessons.title: Design Patterns in Flutter State Management
lessons.theory: ## Reusable Solutions\nDesign patterns solve common architectural problems.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Singleton\n1. Set `pattern` to 'Singleton'.

lessons.title: API Integration Strategies
lessons.theory: ## Connecting Services\nAPIs allow different systems to communicate.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: JSON Data\n1. Set `format` to 'JSON'.

lessons.title: Database Connectivity
lessons.theory: ## Data Access\nConnecting to a DB safely is essential.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Connection String\n1. Set `conn_str` to 'postgres://localhost'.

lessons.title: Scaling Applications
lessons.theory: ## Horizontal Scaling\nAdding more instances handles increased load.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Instance Count\n1. Set `instances` to 3.

lessons.title: Microservices Architecture
lessons.theory: ## Decoupling\nMicroservices break large apps into independent services.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Service Name\n1. Set `service` to 'Auth'.

lessons.title: Best Practices and Anti-patterns
lessons.theory: ## Writing Clean Code\nMaintainability ensures the long-term success of Flutter State Management projects.
lessons.instructions: ## Task: Clean Code\n1. Set `is_clean` to True.
